mapping.a_element: spectral wall (a critical threshold that prevents/enables formation of bound states)
mapping.b_element: sharp decay threshold s_c separating well-posedness from ill-posedness

mapping.a_element: shape normal-mode resonances of the vortex
mapping.b_element: high-low-low-high resonant configuration on the resonant manifold

brief_justification: The machinery differs: paper A is finite-dimensional effective dynamics of interacting topological solitons (inter-vortex forces, centrifugal terms, orbits, spectral walls from mode-to-mode energy transfer in a classical field theory), while paper B is functional-analytic well-posedness theory for a four-wave kinetic PDE (trilinear L^infinity estimates and ill-posedness by concentration near a resonant manifold). The only overlap is generic vocabulary ('threshold', 'resonance', 'modes'); there is no role-for-role correspondence between a dynamical barrier in soliton phase space and a critical regularity exponent, so no shared transferable machinery holds.
